implementation.canonical_literal_path: Before the existing structural simple-inline-string matcher, the reader recognizes exact <is><t>...</t></is> and <is><t xml:space="preserve">...</t></is> payloads with one raw-text terminator search and one literal suffix check.
implementation.safe_fallback: Attribute variants, self-closing text, rich or unsupported nested markup, namespace variants, malformed candidates and reader-window splits continue through the prior structural parser and retain its diagnostics.
implementation.lifetime_and_memory: The fast path returns a callback-lifetime view into the current bounded reader window, copies no payload and retains no string_view after window consumption.
implementation.telemetry: Schema v12 adds canonical inline-string count and bytes beneath the existing aggregate simple-inline-string count, bytes and fallback telemetry; matrix v6 validates positive canonical traffic for mixed-inline and zero traffic for non-inline guards.

guards.timing_policy: Guard elapsed values remain in their matrix artifacts but are excluded from performance claims because unrelated local jobs caused system-sensitive scheduling variation during those runs.

interpretation.supported_claim: In the recorded same-machine back-to-back mixed-inline A/B over byte-identical source workbooks, schema v12 reduced total editor, transform and source scan/action medians by 14.36%, 24.71% and 22.62% relative to the exact schema-v11 baseline binary.
interpretation.coverage: All 333327 completed simple inline-string payloads used the canonical literal path; six reader-window candidates retained the structural fallback. Numeric and formula-plus-hyperlink guards reported zero canonical traffic.
interpretation.memory: Median process peak working set changed by approximately +0.004 MB, so the recorded gain did not require materialized worksheet state or payload copies.
interpretation.compatibility: Representative outputs opened with openpyxl 3.1.2, and the formula guard preserved the recorded external hyperlink target.
interpretation.next_hotspot: After the canonical mixed-inline parser improvement, source scan/action still occupies approximately 0.573 seconds in the optimized A/B median. Further work should profile generic worksheet tag and cell-boundary scanning before changing compression or introducing parallelism.

scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, one-million-cell 10-column deterministic workloads, 1000 targeted upserts, source DEFLATE level 6 and output DEFLATE level 1. The claim is limited to the recorded back-to-back mixed-inline A/B and does not generalize to arbitrary workbooks, Office, cross-machine performance, full-feature superiority or OpenXLSX superiority.
